---
title: "percentile_rescale"
generated: true
description: "Shift by the `lo`-percentile then scale by the `hi`-percentile."
---

[‹ back to `nitrix.numerics.normalize`](../nitrix-numerics-normalize.qmd)

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:percentile_rescale -->
```python
percentile_rescale(x: Num[Array, '...'], *, lo: float = 0.0, hi: float = 99.0, clip: bool = True, axis: Optional[_AxisArg] = None, eps: float = 1e-12, mask: Optional[Num[Array, '...']] = None) -> Num[Array, '...']
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:percentile_rescale -->
Shift by the `lo`-percentile then scale by the `hi`-percentile.

Computes $(x - p_{lo}) / p_{hi}$ where $p_{lo}$ /
$p_{hi}$ are the `lo` / `hi` percentiles of `x`,
then optionally clips the result to $[0, 1]$ when `clip`
is set.  With the defaults `lo=0` (the minimum) and `hi=99`
this is the **min--p99--clip** recipe used by SynthStrip /
SynthDist / SynthSR (and SynthSR after its CT intensity clip):
$\operatorname{clip}((x - \min) / p_{99}, 0, 1)$.

Distinct from [`intensity_normalize`](../nitrix-numerics-normalize/intensity_normalize.qmd) in two ways, both
deliberate:

1. The scale is the **raw high percentile** $p_{hi}$ (not
   the inter-percentile width $p_{hi} - p_{lo}$), so the
   upper reference is an absolute intensity, not a range
   endpoint.
2. It rescales **then** clips ([`intensity_normalize`](../nitrix-numerics-normalize/intensity_normalize.qmd) clips
   then rescales), so values above $p_{hi}$ saturate at
   `1` rather than defining the top of the range.

Use [`intensity_normalize`](../nitrix-numerics-normalize/intensity_normalize.qmd) for symmetric two-sided
percentile-window rescaling; use this for the strict
min/high-percentile convention the Synth* pipelines expect.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:percentile_rescale -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|------------|--------|--------|----------|--------------------------------------------------------------|
| `x` | `Num` | `...` | — | Intensity tensor. |
| `lo` | `float` | — | `0.0` | Lower / upper percentiles. Defaults `(0, 99)` (minimum and 99th percentile). |
| `hi` | `float` | — | `99.0` | Lower / upper percentiles. Defaults `(0, 99)` (minimum and 99th percentile). |
| `clip` | `bool` | — | `True` | Clip the result to `[0, 1]`. Default `True`. |
| `axis` | `Optional[_AxisArg]` | — | `None` | Axes over which to compute the percentiles. `None` (default) uses the whole tensor; pass an axis tuple for per-slice / per-channel rescaling. |
| `eps` | `float` | — | `1e-12` | Stabiliser added to the `p_hi` denominator (guards the all-constant / all-zero input). |
| `mask` | `Optional[Num[Array, '...']]` | — | `None` | Optional foreground mask (same shape as `x`, or broadcastable): when given, the percentiles are computed over the masked (e.g. non-zero / in-brain) voxels only -- the skull-strip-aware variant -- via `nanpercentile`, falling back to the global min / max where a slice has no foreground. Every voxel is still rescaled (and clipped); only the *reference* percentiles change. Pass `mask = x != 0` for the non-zero recipe. |

**Returns** `Num` — shape `...`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen sections:percentile_rescale -->
::: {.callout-note}
The two `jnp.percentile` calls are *not* worth fusing into one
length-2 quantile call: XLA already CSEs the two identical sorts
into a single `lax.sort` (see `intensity_normalize` Notes and
`docs/feature-requests/median-percentile-cpu-sort-cliff.md`).
:::
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->


<!-- nitrix:gen dims:percentile_rescale -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `...` | anonymous leading (batch) axes |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->
